meta.name: Ethiopian Gomen (Collard Greens)
meta.subtitle: Slow-sautéed collards with ginger, garlic, and gentle warm spice—classic side for injera and stews.

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Canonical SSA gomen built for deterministic planning: simple ingredients, clear steps, and adaptable spice levels.
This version is berbere-optional (many homes keep gomen mild alongside spicy wats).
For best texture: strip tough stems, slice thin, and steam-sauté before adding fat.

summary: Gomen is a classic Ethiopian collard greens side, gently cooked with onions, garlic, and ginger, often finished with niter kibbeh or neutral oil.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a side on an injera platter.

required.label: Large skillet or pot with lid

optional.label: Salad spinner
optional.notes: Helps dry washed greens quickly for cleaner sautéing.

notes: Dairy only if using niter kibbeh (spiced clarified butter). Use oil for dairy-free/vegan.

ingredients.label: Collard greens, stems removed, thinly sliced
notes: Sub: kale, chard, or cabbage (adjust cook time).
Slice thin for faster, more even cooking.

ingredients.label: Niter kibbeh (Ethiopian spiced clarified butter)
notes: Classic flavor. If unavailable, use olive oil or neutral oil.
If using salted niter kibbeh, reduce added salt.

ingredients.label: Olive oil (or neutral oil)
notes: Use this if skipping niter kibbeh, or split the fat between oil + niter kibbeh.

ingredients.label: Kosseret (Ethiopian herb) or dried oregano
ingredients.notes: Kosseret is traditional; oregano is a workable substitute.

ingredients.label: Berbere spice blend (optional)
notes: Many gomen versions are mild; add berbere if you want a warmer, spicier side.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; greens vary in bitterness.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Used to steam-sauté the greens so they soften without scorching.
If greens are very wet after washing, reduce added water.
Avoid high heat early—onions should soften and sweeten without browning heavily.
Steam first, then finish in fat: this keeps greens tender and prevents a greasy sauce.
Gomen thickens as it cools; reheat gently with a splash of water.

changes.swap: Use 700–800 g shredded cabbage instead of collards.

changes.add: Add 300 g diced Yukon gold potatoes; parboil 6–8 min first, then add during steaming stage.
changes.note: Increase salt slightly and add 60–120 ml more water as needed.

changes.add: Use 1–2 tbsp berbere and add a pinch more black pepper.
changes.note: Bloom berbere in the fat for 30 seconds before adding onions.

notes: Cool quickly, store airtight, reheat gently with a splash of water.
Texture softens after freezing. Best if you plan to use as a stew-side rather than a crisp vegetable.

steps.title: Prep the greens
instructions: Wash collard greens thoroughly and dry (a little moisture is fine).
Strip leaves from thick stems and discard or save stems for stock.
Stack leaves, roll into a tight bundle, and slice thin ribbons.
checks: Greens are stemmed and sliced into thin ribbons for even cooking.

steps.title: Sweat onions (start the flavor base)
instructions: Heat a large skillet/pot over medium heat.
Add oil (and niter kibbeh if using).
Add diced onion and a small pinch of salt; cook 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and translucent.

steps.title: Add garlic and ginger (and optional spices)
instructions: Add minced garlic and grated ginger; stir 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
If using berbere, add it now and stir 30 seconds to bloom.
checks: Aromatics smell fragrant; nothing is burning.

steps.title: Steam-braise the greens until tender
instructions: Add sliced collards in handfuls, stirring as they wilt.
Add water, cover with a lid, and reduce heat to medium-low.
Cook 18–25 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes, until greens are tender.
If the pan gets dry before greens are tender, add 2–4 tbsp water as needed.
checks: Greens are tender and evenly seasoned; no scorching on the bottom.

steps.title: Reduce, season, and finish
instructions: Remove the lid and cook 2–5 minutes to reduce any excess liquid (greens should be moist but not soupy).
Season with remaining salt and optional black pepper to taste.
Optional: stir in lemon juice off heat to brighten.
checks: Greens are moist, not watery; flavor is savory with gentle warmth.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve warm as part of an injera platter alongside wats, lentils, and salads.
Store leftovers airtight; reheat gently with a splash of water if needed.
